lesson.menu_desc: Deliver your findings aloud to an audience -- walk through the report, emphasise key points, and use the first conditional for recommendations -- 27 slides
lesson.subtitle: Aula 19 -- Presenting Your Report -- Delivering Findings Aloud
lesson.title_tag: Professor View -- Percival JR | Aula 19 -- Presenting Your Report

listenings.text: Hi Percival, this is Karen. You wrote a clear report last week -- now you will deliver it aloud to the board, so let me coach you on the presentation. First, greet the audience and state the objective in one sentence: the objective of this report is to review the March audit. Then walk through the report section by section, in the same order as the headings: Objective, Findings, Conclusion. In the Findings, do not read every number. Point out only the key figures, and emphasise the two high risks. Keep your sentences short and concise, so the audience can follow you. A visual helps a lot here: show one simple chart, and a short handout with the three headings, so everyone can read along. When you reach the Conclusion, give your recommendation with the first conditional: if we approve this control, the risk will drop; if the board agrees, we will start next month. Finish by inviting questions. Remember, you are not reading the report, you are delivering it -- look up, speak slowly, and walk the audience through the story of the audit.

Good morning, everyone, and thank you for your time. The objective of this report is to review the safety audit for March. I will walk you through three sections: Objective, Findings, and Conclusion. First, the Findings. A survey was carried out on three sites, and twelve risks were identified. I will not read every number, so let me point out the key figures: two risks are high, and the data was checked twice, so it is accurate. You can follow the chart on the visual, and there is a short handout in front of you with the three headings. Now the Conclusion. I want to emphasise one recommendation. If we approve the new control, the risk will drop quickly. If the board agrees today, we will start the work next month. To be concise: the audit is complete, two high risks remain, and one control will fix them. Thank you. I am happy to take any questions now.

extra_audio.text: First, Percival greets the audience and states the objective in one short sentence. Then he walks through the report section by section, in the order of the headings. After that, in the Findings, he points out only the key figures and emphasises the two high risks, while the audience follows the visual and the handout. Later, in the Conclusion, he gives his recommendation with the first conditional: if the board agrees, the work will start next month. Finally, he keeps every sentence concise, invites questions, and thanks the audience.
